basics.name: Alexander Friedrichsen
basics.label: Applied AI Engineer
basics.summary: Applied AI engineer who brings competitive intensity to production systems. World Champion card game player turned AI builder. Deployed a fine-tuned GPT vision pipeline projected to save $3M+ annually; designed a 12-agent orchestration platform with autoresearch, model routing, RAG-based retrieval, and automated quality gates. My skillset specializes in making correct generalizations from insufficient data. Published ML researcher (GECCO '23), full-stack Python/TypeScript/cloud. Open to relocation (NYC, SF).

work.name: Union Mutual
work.summary: AI lead at a mutual insurance company. Built production LLM systems and drove organization-wide AI adoption.
highlights: Co-designed and deployed an AI inspection evaluation pipeline — fine-tuned GPT vision model processes property images against underwriter-designed rubrics, outputs structured risk assessments, and generates underwriter reports — projected to save $3M+ annually. Runs in automated batch mode across policies and on-demand per underwriter request
Restructured production codebases for LLM agent integration; established CI/CD, version control (GitHub), and isolated dev/prod environments

Led company-wide AI adoption: trained analytics team on agentic coding tools (Cursor, custom agents, MCP hooks), delivered company-wide lunch-and-learn demos, and represented the company at an agentic AI conference
Conduct exploratory modeling, feature engineering, and SQL querying across AWS (App Runner, Redshift, S3) to support business decision-making

work.name: Team Handshake Moxfield
highlights: Won the Wonders of the First World Championship; Top 8 at Pro Tour Karlov Manor (Magic: The Gathering, $500K prize pool events)
Built MTG Meta Analyzer — full-stack web app (JavaScript, Supabase, PostgreSQL) analyzing 20,000+ competitive match outcomes with ELO rating system, deployed at honestafblog.com
Designs and leads testing process for 16-hour/day, 7-day testing sprints ahead of major tournament events
Implemented Openclaw bot for automated organization and data gathering across team testing process
Brokered team sponsorships and coordinated international travel logistics for 15-person competitive roster

work.name: BAE Systems
highlights: Built multi-source Power BI data models with DAX aggregations, delivering weekly dashboards that drove labor and product line decisions for senior stakeholders
Architected internal staffing solutions using SharePoint and MS Access, serving 16,000 employees
Spearheaded ETL pipeline integrating financial data, enabling transition from manual Excel analysis to predictive analytics

work.name: Social Ecological Gaming and Simulation Lab
highlights: Developed precision agriculture simulation software using Python, C#, and JavaScript in Unity Game Engine

work.name: Vermont Department of Health
highlights: Automated reporting workflows (SAS, R) and published data products for CDC reporting systems, saving 10+ hours per month

education.institution: University of Vermont, Complex Systems Center
University of Vermont, College of Engineering and Mathematics

publications.name: Evolutionary Machine Learning for Robust Facility Placements
publications.summary: Published research on evolutionary algorithms for optimization under uncertainty. Also presented at JuliaCon.

projects.name: Multi-Agent AI Orchestration System
projects.description: Built a production multi-agent system with 12 specialized AI agents coordinated through an Obsidian knowledge vault. Implements RAG-based knowledge retrieval over 200+ documents, model routing across inference tiers for cost-optimized token usage, and structured output parsing for agent coordination.
highlights: 12 specialized agents with role-based task delegation, automated quality gates, and persistent memory
RAG pipeline: BM25 + vector search over knowledge vault, context injection at query time
MCP tool servers, Discord-based always-on infrastructure, custom hooks for secret detection and verification
